headline: Shares of several utility companies are trading lower amid market weakness as stocks sell-off amid COVID-19 concerns, a slower-than-expected vaccine rollout, and profit-taking following a 2020 surge in equities.
summary: Shares of several utility companies are trading lower amid market weakness as stocks sell-off amid COVID-19 concerns, a slower-than-expected vaccine rollout, and profit-taking following a 2020 surge in equities.

headline: Barclays Maintains Overweight on Entergy, Raises Price Target to $124
summary: Barclays  analyst Nicholas Campanella   maintains Entergy (NYSE:ETR) with a Overweight and raises the price target from $119 to $124.

raw.headline: Barclays Maintains Overweight on Entergy, Raises Price Target to $124

headline: Wells Fargo Maintains Overweight on Entergy, Raises Price Target to $128
summary: Wells Fargo  analyst Shahriar Pourreza   maintains Entergy (NYSE:ETR) with a Overweight and raises the price target from $123 to $128.

headline: Meta deal adds to Entergy’s $57B, 4-year capital plan
summary: Entergy’s 2026-2029 capital plan is more than 30% higher than what the company announced three months ago, and it has a pipeline of 7-12 GW of potential data center load.

raw.headline: Meta deal adds to Entergy’s $57B, 4-year capital plan

headline: Entergy Q1 Earnings Call Highlights
summary: Entergy (NYSE:ETR) reported first-quarter adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $0.86 and used its earnings call to detail a major new electric service agreement (ESA) with Meta, a higher long-term retail sales outlook, and a significantly expanded capital plan tied largely to serving new large-load
